2026-04-22T16:33:35.6659757Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-22T16:33:35.6660295Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-22T16:33:35.6660975Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-22T16:33:35.6661343Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-22T16:33:35.6661652Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-22T16:33:35.6661897Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-22T16:33:35.6662066Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-22T16:33:35.6662256Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-22T16:33:35.6662429Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-22T16:33:35.6662668Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-22T16:33:35.6662874Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-22T16:33:35.6663159Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-22T16:33:35.6663420Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

2026-04-22T16:37:31.2876151Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-22T16:35:11.019380038Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58505,\"title\":\"[ComputeLimit] Docs: Add service-specific documentation, key concepts, and code samples to Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeLimit RE\\n[Content truncated due to length]\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeLimit`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/computelimit/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeLimit/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit 598a6b22 (#57628) by `@azure-sdk`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nCommit [598a6b22](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/commit/[REDACTED]) added the `Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeLimit` package — a brand-new management-plane SDK (version 1.0.0) for the Azure Compute Limit service, which enables subscriptions to share compute quota limits. The CHANGELOG documents the following new resources and operations:\\n\\n- `ComputeLimitVmFamilyResource`, `ComputeLimitVmFamilyCollection`, `ComputeLimitVmFamilyData` — managing VM family resources\\n- `Disable` operation on `ComputeLimitFeatureResource`\\n- `ComputeLimitGuestSubscriptionResource` and `ComputeLimitSharedLimitResource` exist in the generated code but are not described anywhere\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **README contains only boilerplate text** — no service description, no resource model overview, no service-specific getting started guidance\\n- **Key Concepts section is missing** — the four resource types (`ComputeLimitFeatureResource`, `ComputeLimitVmFamilyResource`, `ComputeLimitGuestSubscriptionResource`, `ComputeLimitSharedLimitResource`) and the shared-quota domain model are not explained anywhere in the docs\\n- **Examples section has no real code** — only a generic link to `aka.ms/azuresdk-net-mgmt-samples`; no package-specific snippet for even the most basic scenario (list VM families, enable/disable a feature, add a guest subscription)\\n- **No `tests/Samples/` directory exists** — there are no test-backed snippets to populate the README via `Update-Snippets.ps1`\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File: `sdk/computelimit/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeLimit/README.md`** — edit (replace the boilerplate body while keeping the header and links structure)\\n\\nReplace the **Key concepts** and **Examples** sections with content describing the service domain and real resource usage:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nAzure Compute Limit (Microsoft.ComputeLimit) lets a host subscription share its compute quota with guest subscriptions. Key resource types in this library:\\n\\n| Resource | Description |\\n|---|---|\\n| `ComputeLimitFeatureResource` | Represents the Compute Limit feature on a subscription. Use `Enable` / `Disable` to opt a subscription in or out. |\\n| `ComputeLimitSharedLimitResource` | Defines the maximum amount of quota a host subscription will share. |\\n| `ComputeLimitGuestSubscriptionResource` | Represents a guest subscription that has been granted access to the host\\u0026#39;s shared quota. |\\n| `ComputeLimitVmFamilyResource` | Represents a specific VM family within a location for which quota sharing is configured. |\\n\\nAll resources are accessed through extension methods on `SubscriptionResource` from `Azure.ResourceManager`.\\n```\\n\\nReplace the **Examples** section:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\nThe following examples illustrate common operations. For a complete list of code samples, see the\\n[.NET Management Library Code Samples]((aka.ms/redacted)\\n\\n### Authenticate and create a client\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### List VM families in a location\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Enable the Compute Limit feature on a subscription\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Add a guest subscription\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nCreate a new test-backed sample file:\\n\\n**File: `sdk/computelimit/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeLimit/tests/Samples/Sample1_ComputeLimitOperations.cs`** — create\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System;\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\nusing Azure.Identity;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeLimit;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeLimit.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public class Sample1_ComputeLimitOperations\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task AuthenticateAndCreateClient()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:ComputeLimit_AuthClient\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            #endregion\\n            await Task.CompletedTask;\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task ListVmFamilies()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:ComputeLimit_ListVmFamilies\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            string subscriptionId = Environment.GetEnvironmentVariable(\\u0026#34;AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID\\u0026#34;);\\n            var subscription = client.GetSubscriptionResource(\\n                SubscriptionResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(subscriptionId));\\n\\n            AzureLocation location = AzureLocation.EastUS;\\n            var vmFamilies = subscription.GetComputeLimitVmFamilies(location);\\n            await foreach (var vmFamily in vmFamilies.GetAllAsync())\\n            {\\n                Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;VM Family: {vmFamily.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task EnableComputeLimitFeature()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:ComputeLimit_EnableFeature\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            string subscriptionId = Environment.GetEnvironmentVariable(\\u0026#34;AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID\\u0026#34;);\\n            var subscription = client.GetSubscriptionResource(\\n                SubscriptionResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(subscriptionId));\\n\\n            var featureCollection = subscription.GetComputeLimitFeatures();\\n            // Enable the feature for this subscription\\n            var feature = await featureCollection.GetAsync(\\u0026#34;default\\u0026#34;);\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Feature state: {feature.Value.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task AddGuestSubscription()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:ComputeLimit_AddGuestSubscription\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            string subscriptionId = Environment.GetEnvironmentVariable(\\u0026#34;AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID\\u0026#34;);\\n            var subscription = client.GetSubscriptionResource(\\n                SubscriptionResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(subscriptionId));\\n\\n            var guestSubscriptions = subscription.GetComputeLimitGuestSubscriptions();\\n            await foreach (var guest in guestSubscriptions.GetAllAsync())\\n            {\\n                Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Guest subscription: {guest.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\nFor each snippet, the README placeholder (added in Step 1) uses these snippet names:\\n- `ComputeLimit_AuthClient` → \\u0026#34;Authenticate and create a client\\u0026#34; section\\n- `ComputeLimit_ListVmFamilies` → \\u0026#34;List VM families in a location\\u0026#34; section\\n- `ComputeLimit_EnableFeature` → \\u0026#34;Enable the Compute Limit feature on a subscription\\u0026#34; section\\n- `ComputeLimit_AddGuestSubscription` → \\u0026#34;Add a guest subscription\\u0026#34; section\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at snippet placeholders (provided above)\\n4. ✅ **Troubleshooting** — present and adequate\\n5. ✅ **Next steps** — present and adequate\\n6. ✅ **Contributing** — present and adequate\\n\\n### Step 4: The CHANGELOG documents the following new resources and operations:\\n\\n- `ComputeLimitVmFamilyResource`, `ComputeLimitVmFamilyCollection`, `ComputeLimitVmFamilyData` — managing VM family resources\\n- `Disable` operation on `ComputeLimitFeatureResource`\\n- `ComputeLimitGuestSubscriptionResource` and `ComputeLimitSharedLimitResource` exist in the generated code but are not described anywhere\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **README contains only boilerplate text** — no service description, no resource model overview, no service-specific getting started guidance\\n- **Key Concepts section is missing** — the four resource types (`ComputeLimitFeatureResource`, `ComputeLimitVmFamilyResource`, `ComputeLimitGuestSubscriptionResource`, `ComputeLimitSharedLimitResource`) and the shared-quota domain model are not explained anywhere in the docs\\n- **Examples section has no real code** — only a generic link to `aka.ms/azuresdk-net-mgmt-samples`; no package-specific snippet for even the most basic scenario (list VM families, enable/disable a feature, add a guest subscription)\\n- **No `tests/Samples/` directory exists** — there are no test-backed snippets to populate the README via `Update-Snippets.ps1`\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File: `sdk/computelimit/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeLimit/README.md`** — edit (replace the boilerplate body while keeping the header and links structure)\\n\\nReplace the **Key concepts** and **Examples** sections with content describing the service domain and real resource usage:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nAzure Compute Limit (Microsoft.ComputeLimit) lets a host subscription share its compute quota with guest subscriptions. Key resource types in this library:\\n\\n| Resource | Description |\\n|---|---|\\n| `ComputeLimitFeatureResource` | Represents the Compute Limit feature on a subscription. Use `Enable` / `Disable` to opt a subscription in or out. |\\n| `ComputeLimitSharedLimitResource` | Defines the maximum amount of quota a host subscription will share. |\\n| `ComputeLimitGuestSubscriptionResource` | Represents a guest subscription that has been granted access to the host\\u0026#39;s shared quota. |\\n| `ComputeLimitVmFamilyResource` | Represents a specific VM family within a location for which quota sharing is configured. |\\n\\nAll resources are accessed through extension methods on `SubscriptionResource` from `Azure.ResourceManager`.\\n```\\n\\nReplace the **Examples** section:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\nThe following examples illustrate common operations. Snippet:ComputeLimit_ListVmFamilies\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            string subscriptionId = Environment.GetEnvironmentVariable(\\u0026#34;AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID\\u0026#34;);\\n            var subscription = client.GetSubscriptionResource(\\n                SubscriptionResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(subscriptionId));\\n\\n            AzureLocation location = AzureLocation.EastUS;\\n            var vmFamilies = subscription.GetComputeLimitVmFamilies(location);\\n            await foreach (var vmFamily in vmFamilies.GetAllAsync())\\n            {\\n                Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;VM Family: {vmFamily.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task EnableComputeLimitFeature()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:ComputeLimit_EnableFeature\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            string subscriptionId = Environment.GetEnvironmentVariable(\\u0026#34;AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID\\u0026#34;);\\n            var subscription = client.GetSubscriptionResource(\\n                SubscriptionResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(subscriptionId));\\n\\n            var featureCollection = subscription.GetComputeLimitFeatures();\\n            // Enable the feature for this subscription\\n            var feature = await featureCollection.GetAsync(\\u0026#34;default\\u0026#34;);\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Feature state: {feature.Value.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task AddGuestSubscription()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:ComputeLimit_AddGuestSubscription\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            string subscriptionId = Environment.GetEnvironmentVariable(\\u0026#34;AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID\\u0026#34;);\\n            var subscription = client.GetSubscriptionResource(\\n                SubscriptionResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(subscriptionId));\\n\\n            var guestSubscriptions = subscription.GetComputeLimitGuestSubscriptions();\\n            await foreach (var guest in guestSubscriptions.GetAllAsync())\\n            {\\n                Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Guest subscription: {guest.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\nFor each snippet, the README placeholder (added in Step 1) uses these snippet names:\\n- `ComputeLimit_AuthClient` → \\u0026#34;Authenticate and create a client\\u0026#34;
